posts.text: Counsel Health is THE hottest health tech company in NYC and Boston. Remote friendly! I get the privilege of partnering with Angel Maredia as we scale the product management team at Counsel. We’re hiring two Sr Product Managers! Sr Product Manager, Consumer - https://lnkd.in/eqWqt2DM You own the experience patients have with their AI-enabled doctor. Every onboarding flow, every care interaction, every clinical touchpoint is yours to shape. Sr Product Manager, Enterprise - https://lnkd.in/e_xzs3uz You decide how Counsel shows up inside the world’s leading healthcare organizations. Equal parts platform architect and patient advocate. I’m pumped! DMs are open. Let’s build.
posts.hook: Counsel Health is THE hottest health tech company in NYC and Boston.
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: Big news coming out of Counsel Health! Starting June 16, ŌURA members can go from a real biometric signal to physician-led care in minutes, inside the same app they already use. No switching. No leaving your house to wait at urgent care. No starting from scratch with a doctor who doesn't know your history. The wearable space has gotten incredibly good at surfacing data. What's always been missing is a fast, trustworthy path to actually do something with it. That's exactly what we're building at Counsel and this partnership is a big step in that direction. Proud of the team. Fired up for what's next with Counsel! Were also hiring! ❤️ Check out our open jobs here - https://lnkd.in/eiGxyP4B Read the full announcement ➡️ https://lnkd.in/eTRD36WD More to come!
posts.hook: Big news coming out of Counsel Health!
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: We're growing fast at Counsel Health, and the clinical side of the business is leading it. Our physicians practice medicine the way most have always wanted to: seeing patients, using judgment, and doing the part only they can do. The difference is how. Visits are messaging-based, AI-paired, and built around what's actually best for the patient instead of what the EMR will let you click. We're hiring across the clinical org right now: - Physicians, Virtual Care: full time, part time, and evenings/weekends. If you want to practice in a care model built from scratch around the clinician, this is it. - Senior Talent Partner, Clinical: we're building the team behind the team. This role owns physician recruiting end to end as we scale. If any of these fit someone in your network, I'd love an intro!!! Physicians Virtual Care (Full Time) — https://lnkd.in/ebEB95kR Physicians Virtual Care (Part Time) — https://lnkd.in/eK5Hx2Uv Physicians Virtual Care (Evenings/Weekends) — https://lnkd.in/ewpVkMCB Senior Talent Partner, Clinical — https://lnkd.in/eAZQ-wdW
posts.hook: We're growing fast at Counsel Health, and the clinical side of the business is leading it.
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: I found a company actually doing something about it. I'm joining Counsel Health as their first Head of Talent and I could not be more fired up about it. Here's the thing about healthcare access. When something feels wrong, you're stuck choosing between googling your symptoms, asking Claude, calling your doctor who can't see you for three weeks, or ending up in an urgent care or ER that costs a fortune. Nobody wins in that scenario, and the honest truth is that sometimes every single day counts. My family deserves fast, real answers when something is wrong. So does yours. Counsel is fixing that. It's the best of both worlds. Medical grade AI combined with physician oversight, delivering answers in minutes instead of weeks. Backed by Andreessen Horowitz and GV (Google Ventures), they're already serving over 100,000 members nationwide, and the growth is just getting started. When I met Muthu Alagappan, MD, Founder and CEO, I walked away thinking this guy is building something that actually matters and is going to make a difference in the world. The mission is real, the traction speaks for itself, and the team is exactly the kind of people I want to go to work with every day. The opportunity to build the talent function at a company like this from the ground up is exactly the kind of work I want to be doing. 20 years in TA and HR, a consulting chapter that sharpened me in ways I didn't expect, and now this. Grateful to Amit Bhatt for believing in me and making it happen. To Justin Yu, Ann Kelly, Julia Siu, and Leah Nida for running a great process, I'm psyched to be partnering with you! And to McKenzie Furber at GV for the intro that started it all. Let's go.
posts.hook: I found a company actually doing something about it.
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: "Closing time. Every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end." Door3 Talent has been one of the most creatively alive periods of my career. The AI strategy work, the training and enablement, the building. Especially the building in public. I got to work with companies I genuinely cared about, people I respected, and problems worth solving. 22 invoices sent and collected. Clients and partners that trusted me to show up and deliver: TA and People Teams at Bottomline, Career Pathways Initiative, Wistia, EvenUp, ActiveCampaign, Liberate, Rapid7, People Innovations, Workable, Manifold, Synopsys Inc., and Bond Global. Thank you! Its been a busy year. Lets just say, I've been hustling. Work ranged from AI skills training and talent strategy to executive placements and embedded HR/Talent leadership. A few honest things I learned as a solopreneur: - Selling and doing at the same time is hard. Like, actually really hard. - Your reputation is your only sales team. Every deliverable is a reference. - Building in public compounds. The tools, the content, the community. That flywheel is uber real. - The silence is louder than you expect. - And then the honest part nobody talks about enough, it's lonely. I'd do it again with a partner. But solo, there's a ceiling on what you can feel good about, not just what you can produce. - I'm also a leader. That's not ego, it's just how I'm wired. Leaders need a team. People to invest in, to develop, to go to battle with. That's not available when you're a company of one. I missed it more than I expected to and it took me a bit longer to recognize. Special thank you to Kim Schofield at Bottomline for being my very first training client. You didn't know it at the time (well maybe you did), but that first invoice meant everything. And to Elizabeth Gormley at Rapid7 for being my first AI strategy engagement. That one set the tone for everything that came after. As one door closes... I've never felt healthier about a decision in my life. More to come on that next week. It's time to shut the Door.
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: Big week ahead. Wednesday I'm at Foundation Medicine in Boston with the House of Visionaries Community crew. Morning is the CPO Brunch where I'm co-presenting with Andrew Suchoff on a topic near and dear, personal branding. Then the afternoon session runs 12:30 to 4:30 with Rachel Salamone from Boston Dynamics, Andrew Suchoff and Ian Ide from Foundation Medicine, Katie Kulikoski and Stephanie Steele from Rapid7, and Ben Rutter covering Quality and Impact of Hire and How to Think Like a CEO. Two killer topics! Oh, and there's a mystery guest. Born in Boston. Icon of its industry. Truly visionary. I have theories. Anyone have ideas? Thursday I'm presenting at Maven AGI for the Scale event. Invite-only, 30-40 senior People leaders from Boston-area startups and scale-ups. The theme is Moments that Matter, automate the predictable and create wow in the moments that truly count. The agenda is stacked. Our gracious host, Lauren Murphy and team from Maven are opening with an AI Problem/Solve, the amazing Kat Cortave is presenting on building an AI agent and what she learned along the way, Amy Kruglak, SPHR from Elucid is spotlighting how they're automating people processes, and I'm up with a 20-minute talk on the lifecycle of AI tools and ROI. Then Tom Foreman runs a full interactive workshop on the future of AI and humans. Happy hour after. This is exactly the kind of room I want to be in. Shoutout to the Bond Global team for putting this all together, Ben, Christina, Tom, Laura, Sabrina, and Ryan. You build rooms worth being in!!! And to my fellow board members at House of Visionaries, Christina, Carrie, and Katie, big ups! See you there!
posts.name: Michael Brown

posts.text: All good projects come to an end. Last week I wrapped up with Chris McMahon and the Manifold AI team. Chris, working with you this past year has been one of the better professional relationships I've had. We built a ton!!!! We figured a lot out and somewhere in there it stopped feeling like consulting and started feeling like partnership. I'm proud of what we accomplished and where Manifold is today. I'll genuinely miss it. Thank you! This week I wrap up at Synopsys Inc. A few months in, but dense ones to be honest. This was a serious AI strategy engagement on a serious set of opportunities, and I believe the team is now in a great spot to continue pushing their strategy forward. Natalie, I enjoyed our chats, you are an incredible TA Leader. Thank you for making each week better than the last. Rob Devlin, I don't know how two bald, bearded guys from opposite sides of the Atlantic end up being the same person, but here we are. Sneakers, Wu-Tang, AI, Dogs, Empathetic leadership!!!! Two peas in a pod. Grateful for both of these engagements. Grateful for the people. More to come this week. Let's just say the next chapter has me fired up.
posts.hook: All good projects come to an end.
posts.name: Michael Brown
